## Summary

This PR fixes **2 HIGH severity information disclosure vulnerabilities** in API error responses:

1. **Request URL Disclosure \(CWE-209\)** - Removed full request URLs from 404 error messages and response bodies
2. **Entity Field Name Enumeration \(CWE-215\)** - Removed all entity field names from validation error messages

## Impact

- ✅ No sensitive data \(API tokens, session IDs, internal IDs\) leaked via error responses
- ✅ No database schema enumeration via field name observation
- ✅ Server-side logging preserved for debugging and security audits
- ✅ 100% backward compatible \(HTTP status codes, error codes unchanged\)

## Testing

- ✅ All 156 tests passing
- ✅ 4 test assertions updated to match new secure behavior
- ✅ No regressions or breaking changes

## Files Changed

- `src/main/java/ipss/web2/examen/exceptions/GlobalExceptionHandler.java` - Fixed 4 exception handlers
- 4 controller test files - Updated assertions

## Security Details

See attached security review report for full vulnerability analysis and remediation details.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)
EOF
\)")
